items.text: Initial Diagnosis: Self-diagnosis performed after the ABS or VSA warning light comes on with the ignition in the ON mode and before the engine is started and the ABS or VSA warning light goes out.
Permanent Diagnostics: Self-diagnostics that continue from the time the ABS or VSA warning light goes off until the ignition is turned to the OFF (LOCK) mode.
When performing diagnostics, be sure to check the "Troubleshooting Procedure" and follow the troubleshooting procedure.
When conducting test drives, the driver must comply with all laws and regulations, observe good manners, and drive in accordance with the surrounding conditions.

rows.text: Common conditions:. 12V battery voltage of 10V or more Typical Methods:. 1. Start the engine and wait at least 5 seconds.
1 drive cycle Brake warning light (red): on ABS warning light: on VSA warning light: on

Common conditions:. Other DTCs not detected Typical Methods:. 1. Start the engine. 2. Drive the vehicle at a speed of 20 km/h or more for at least 6 minutes. 3. Immediately accelerate to a speed of 40 km/h or more and step on the brake to stop the vehicle. (Pay close attention to the surrounding traffic conditions. 4. Repeat steps 2 and 3 three times.
1 drive cycle VSA warning light: on

VSA function stopped TCS function stopped Brake assist function deactivated Creep Aid function deactivated Engine Drag Control function deactivated City Brake Active System deactivated EMERGENCY STOP Signal function stop (depends on case)
